unemployment Rate; The unemployment rate is a measure of the prevalence of unemployment. It is calculated as a percentage by dividing the number of unemployed individuals by all individuals currently in the labor force.
wages Total; Wages include bonuses, stock options, severance pay, profit distributions, cash value of meals and lodging, tips and other gratuities
wages Weekly; Wages include bonuses, stock options, severance pay, profit distributions, cash value of meals and lodging, tips and other gratuities

BLS_In Labor Force; Labor force includes employed and unemployed people. They must be working, receiving unemployment benefits or actively seeking employment.

BLS_Not In Labor Force; Persons who are neither employed nor unemployed are not in the labor force. This category includes retired persons, students, those taking care of children or other family members, and others who are neither working nor seeking work
Not In Labor Force; Persons who are neither employed not looking for work are not in the labor force.
In Labor Force; People who are fulfilling the requirement for inclusion in the employed or unemployed.
BLS_Employed Full Time; Full time employment is considered to be 35+ hours/week.
BLS_Employed Part Time; Part time employment is considered to be 1-34 hours/week.
Adjusted; Indicates the adjustment of time series data on an annual basis.
Unadjusted; Indicates raw time series data without adjustments.
SAMHSA_Employed Other; The Other Employment category includes students, persons keeping house or caring for children full time, retired or disabled persons, or other persons not in the labor force.
Unemployed; The condition of one who is capable of working, actively seeking work, but unable to find any work.

BLSEstablishment; An establishment is a single physical location where one predominant activity occurs. A firm is an establishment or a combination of establishments is defined by its unique Employer Identification number (EIN)

SOCv2018 Enum; The 2018 Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) system is used by federal statistical agencies to classify workers and jobs into occupational categories for the purpose of collecting, calculating, analyzing, or disseminating data.

BLSSeasonally Adjusted; Indicates the adjustment of time series data to eliminate the effect of intrayear variations which tend to occur during the same period on an annual basis
BLSSeasonally Unadjusted; Indicates raw time series data without adjustments to eliminate the effect of intrayear variations.
Job Change Event Enum; Describes different job change events that may happen to workers.
Hire; Describes a worker being hired to a company.
Separation; An event where an employee is separated from the payroll.
Voluntary Separation; Represents employees who left voluntarily. Exceptions are retirements or transfers to other locations, which are considered as 'Other' separations.
Involuntary Separation; Involuntary separations initiated by the employer.
Other Separation; Represents job separations due to retirements, transfers to other locations, deaths, or separations due to employee disability.
A Consumer Price Index measures changes in the price level of a weighted average market basket of consumer goods and services purchased by households.

Consumer; A consumer is a person who intends to order or use purchased goods, products, or services primarily for personal, social, family, household and similar needs, not directly related to entrepreneurial or business activities.
Urban Consumer; The all-urban consumer population consists of all urban households in metropolitan statistical areas (MSAs) and in urban places of 2,500 inhabitants or more.

Urban Wage Earner And Clerical Worker; The urban wage earner and clerical worker population consists of consumer units consisting of clerical workers, sales workers, craft workers, operative, service workers, or laborers.

health Related Social Need; Health-related social needs are the social factors that significantly impact a person's health and well-being, such as housing, transportation, food security, and social support.

particulate Matter2.5; Particulate matter (PM) - is made up of particles (tiny pieces) of solids or liquids that are in the air, like dust, dirt, soot, smoke, and drops of liquid. Fine (smaller) particles, called PM2.5, are more dangerous because they can get into the deep parts of a person's lungs - or even into their blood.
Cervical Cancer Screening; The process of detecting and removing abnormal tissue or cells in the cervix before cervical cancer develops.

palmer Drought Severity Index; The Palmer Drought Severity Index (PDSI) uses readily available temperature and precipitation data to estimate relative dryness.

standardized Precipitation Evapotranspiration Index; The Standardized Precipitation Evapotranspiration Index (SPEI) is an extension of the widely used Standardized Precipitation Index (SPI). The SPEI is designed to take into account both precipitation and potential evapotranspiration (PET) in determining drought.
standardized Precipitation Index; The Standardized Precipitation Index (SPI) is a widely used index to characterize meteorological drought on a range of timescales.
serotype; Serotypes are groups within a single species of microorganisms, such as bacteria or viruses, which share distinctive surface structures.
serogroup; A group of serotypes that have one or more antigens in common

Separated; Includes people legally separated or otherwise absent from their spouse because of marital discord. Those without a final divorce decree are classified as \'separated\'.
Unmarried; Includes all people who are not married at the time of interview (including those who are now divorced, widowed and did not re-marry).
Never Married; Includes all people who have never been married, including people whose only marriage(s) was annulled.
Ever Married; Includes people ever married at the time of interview (including those now married, separated, widowed, or divorced).
Widowed; Includes widows and widowers who have not remarried.
Divorced; Includes people who are legally divorced and who have not remarried. Those without a final divorce decree are classified as \'separated\'.
Now Married; All people whose current marriage has not ended by widowhood or divorce.
Married And Not Separated; Includes people whose current marriage has not ended through widowhood or divorce (regardless of previous marital history), and who are not currently separated.

householder Related Children Under18 Years; Number of related children of the householder under 18 years. A related child is any child under 18 years old who is related to the householder by birth, marriage, or adoption. Related children of the householder include ever married as well as never-married children.

householder Own Children Under18 Years; Number of own children of the householder under 18 years. An own child is a never-married child under 18 years who is a son or daughter by birth, a stepchild, or an adopted child of the householder. In certain tabulations, own children are further classified as living with two parents or with one parent only.

veteran Status; This property identifies if person is a veteran. A veteran is someone who served in the active military, naval or air service, and was discharged or released under conditions other than dishonorable.

payroll Status; Describes whether a business pays employees.
tax Status; In the US, this describes a corporation's tax status with the IRS.
business Operation Type; Describes the type of business operations an establishment is involved with.
owner Race; Identifies the race of majority owner for a firm or business. In case of a single owner, this identifies race of the owner.
owner Gender; Identifies the gender of majority owner for a firm or business. In case of single owner, this identifies gender of the owner.
mortgage Status; The status of the mortgage on a housing unit.
commute Time; The time it takes for a person to get to and from work.
place Of Work; The kind of place where a person works.
resident Status; The status of a person's residence in a place, e.g. \lived in the same house 1 year ago.\
facilities; Facilities available in a building, e.g. plumbing facilities, kitchen facilities.
internet Access Status; The status of a household's internet access.
period Of Military Service; The period that a person served in the military.
property Tax; The amount of property tax paid.
commute Mode; The way a person gets to work, e.g. carpooling or driving alone.
date Built; The date that a building was built.
vehicles Available; Number of vehicles available at a place.

retirement Income; Retirement income includes regular income from a company pension, union pension, Federal government pension, state government pension, local government pension, U.S. military pension, KEOGH retirement plan, SEP (Simplified Employee Pension) or any other type of pension, retirement account or annuity such as IRA, ROTH IRA, 401(k) or 403(b)

social Security Income; Social Security income includes Social Security and U.S. railroad retirement pensions and survivor benefits, permanent disability insurance payments made by the Social Security Administration prior to deductions for medical insurance, and railroad retirement insurance checks from the U.S. government. Medicare reimbursements are not included

supplemental Security Income; Supplemental Security Income (SSI) is a nationwide U.S. assistance program administered by the Social Security Administration that guarantees a minimum level of income for needy aged, blind, or disabled individuals.

cash Assistance; Public assistance income includes general assistance and Temporary Assistance to Needy Families (TANF). Separate payments received for hospital or other medical care (vendor payments) are excluded. This does not include Supplemental Security Income (SSI) or non cash benefits such as Food Stamps.
earnings; Earnings are defined as the sum of wage or salary income and net income from self-employment.
date Of Entry; Used to specify the date when an individual entered a country. Eg: Entered 2000 to 2009
telephone Service Availability; Used to describe if the household has telephone service or not
number Of Occupants; Used to describe the number of people living or sharing a household, housing unit or a room in a housing unit
date Moved In; The date when the householder moved into the housing unit
person Miles Traveled; Number of miles traveled by person in any mode of transportation.
person Trips; Number of trips made by a person in any mode of transportation.
vehicle Miles Traveled; Number of miles a vehicle traveled/covered.
vehicle Trips; Number of trips made by a vehicle.
transportation Type; Transportation type used by Household/Person.
Chemical Substance; A chemical substance is a form of matter having constant chemical composition and characteristic properties.

Chemical Element; A chemical element is a pure substance consisting only of atoms that all have the same numbers of protons in their nuclei and cannot be broken down into simpler substances by any chemical reaction.
Chemical Substance Type Enum; Different types of chemical elements.
Metal; A material that, when freshly prepared, polished, or fractured, shows a lustrous appearance, and conducts electricity and heat relatively well.
Non Metal; Substance that does not display properties of a metal.
Metalloid; Substance that displays a mix of properties of metals and nonmetals.
Alkali Metal; The alkali metals are all shiny, soft, highly reactive metals at standard temperature and pressure and readily lose their outermost electron to form cations with charge +1.
Alkaline Earth Metal; The alkaline earth metals are six chemical elements in group 2 of the periodic table that readily lose electrons to form cations with charge +2.
Semi Metal; A type of chemical element that has properties in between, or that are a mixture of, those of metals and nonmetals.
Heavy Metal; Heavy metals are generally defined as metals with relatively high densities, atomic weights, or atomic numbers.
Halogen; A group of chemical elements that react with metals to produce a wide range of salts.
Noble Gas; Class of chemical elements that are odorless, colorless, monatomic gases with very low chemical reactivity.
Transition Metal; An element whose atom has a partially filled d sub-shell, or which can give rise to cations with an incomplete d sub-shell.
Post Transition Metal; The metallic elements in the periodic table located between the transition metals and the chemically weak nonmetallic metalloids.
Lanthanide; One of the 15 metallic chemical elements with atomic numbers 57–71, from lanthanum through lutetium.
Actinide; One of the 15 metallic chemical elements with atomic numbers from 89 to 103, actinium through lawrencium.
Transactinide; Transactinide  or super-heavy elements, are the chemical elements with atomic numbers greater than 103 which is the heaviest actinide, Lawrencium.
Chalcogen; The chalcogens are the chemical elements in group 16 of the periodic table.
Pnictogen; The Pnictogens are the chemical elements in group 15 of the periodic table, also known as the nitrogen family.
iupac Name; Chemical name as per the International Union of Pure and Applied Chemistry(IUPAC) nomenclature.

molecular Formula; A molecular formula or chemical formula is a way of presenting information about the chemical proportions of atoms that constitute a particular chemical compound or molecule, using chemical element symbols, numbers, and sometimes also other symbols.
atomic Number; The atomic number or proton number (symbol Z) of a chemical element is the number of protons found in the nucleus of every atom of that element.

IPCCPlace_50; A grid on the Earth's surface approximately corresponding to 0.50 degree resolution for attaching climate related observations. These are defined within the context of a specific country.

IPCCPlace_25; A grid on the Earth's surface approximately corresponding to 0.25 degree resolution for attaching climate related observations. These are defined within the context of a specific country.
Air Quality Site; Site for air quality measurements.
Water Quality Site; Site for water quality measurements.
Superfund Site; Superfund sites are polluted locations in the United States requiring a long-term response to clean up hazardous material contaminations.
Superfund Measurement Site; Superfund sites have sampling sites from where the level of contamination is measured over time. Some examples of sampling sites are wells, monitoring stations.
Atmosphere; Layer of gases surrounding a planet.
Sea ice; The ice that floats on the surface of the water.
Ice cover; The physical material that is at the surface of the earth.
Graupel; A small, dry, sandy particle that is formed by the evaporation of water from a hot surface.
Cloud; A visible aerosol consisting of tiny water droplets, ice crystals, or other particles suspended in the atmosphere of a planetary body.
Ice; Water frozen into a solid state. It can appear transparent or a more or less opaque white color, depending on the presence of impurities such as air bubbles or soil.
Rainwater; Water that has fallen as rain and has not yet collected any soluble matter from the soil.
Land Cover; The physical material that is at the surface of the earth.
Tide Gauge Station; A station with a tide guage to continuously record the height of the surrounding water level.
Geo Grid Place_1 Deg; A place representing a uniform 1x1 degree grid on the surface of the Earth. Unlike IPCCPlace entities, these are not defined in the context of a country.
Geo Grid Place_0.25 Deg; A place representing a uniform 0.25x0.25 degree grid on the surface of the Earth.
Geo Grid Place_4 KM; A place representing a uniform 4km grid on the surface of the Earth.
heavy Precipitation Index; Portion of total rainfall within a given time-period that occured on 'extreme' precipitation days (defined as 1st percentile of rainfall)
consecutive Dry Days; Maximum number of consecutive dry days in the given time period
air Quality Site Monitor; Parameter occurrence code for monitor in a Air Quality Site.
defining Pollutant; Pollutant used for aggregate AQI measurement.
defining Air Quality Site; Air Quality Site of aggregate AQI measurement.
pollutant Health Risk; Health risks posed by pollutants
lifetime Contraction Probability; The probability of contracting a disease (like cancer) over a 70 year lifetime from constant, continuous exposure
hazard Index; EPA index where 1 or lower means exposure is unlikely to cause noncancer health effects over lifetime of exposure
temperature; A physical quantity that expresses hot and cold.
wet Bulb Temperature; The temperature read by a thermometer covered in water-soaked cloth (a wet-bulb thermometer) over which air is passed.
precipitation Rate; Amount of precipitation which includes drizzling, rain, sleet, snow, ice pellets, graupel and hail, over time.
climate Model Experiment Type; The type of climate model experiment, e.g. backcast (forecasts in the past) or future forecast of climate indicators, e.g. Temperature.
emissions Scenario; Projections of future greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions used by analysts to assess future vulnerability to climate change.
socioeconomic Scenario; Projections of socio-economic development used by analysts to assess future vulnerability to climate change.
humidity Parameter; The kind of relative humidity parameter used for computing derived climate parameters.
wind Speed; A fundamental atmospheric quantity caused by air moving from high to low pressure, usually due to changes in temperature.
radiation; The emission or transmission of energy in the form of waves or particles through space or through a material medium.
humidity; The concentration of water vapour present in the air.
visibility; The quality or state of being visible.
wavelength; The spatial period of a periodic wave.
radiation Direction; The direction radiation is measured.

water Alkalinity; Alkalinity is the capacity of water to resist acidification. Measuring alkalinity is important in determining a stream's ability to neutralize acidic pollution from rainfall or wastewater
electrical Conductivity; dcid:Electrical Conductivity of water is its ability to conduct an electric current. The purer the water the lower the conductivity.
water Hardness; Water hardness is generally the amount of dissolved calcium and magnesium in water.

potential Of Hydrogen; Potential of Hydrogens (p H) is a quantitative measure of the acidity or basicity of aqueous or other liquid solutions. Solutions with less than 7 are considered acidic whereas the solutions with p H greater than 7 are known as basic.

residual Sodium Carbonate; The residual sodium carbonate (RSC) of water is used to indicate the alkalinity hazard for water. The RSC index is used to find the suitability of the water for irrigation in clay soils.
sodium Adsorption Ratio; The Sodium adsorption ratio (SAR) is an irrigation water quality parameter used in the management of sodium-affected soils.

total Dissolved Solids; Total dissolved solids is a measure of the dissolved combined content of all inorganic and organic substances present in water in molecular, ionized, or micro-granular suspended form.
dissolved Oxygen; Dissolved oxygen refers to the level of free, non-compound oxygen present in water or other liquids.
surface Level; Measure of the surface level of bodies of water, etc.
dew Point Temperature; Temperature to which air must be cooled to become saturated with water vapor, assuming constant air pressure and water content.
precipitable Water; Amount of water in a column of the atmosphere, if the entire column were precipitated as rain.
cloud Cover; The fraction of the sky obscured by clouds.
wind Component; The component of wind vector, which might be one of U, V or Z.","Wind shear can be broken down into vertical and horizontal components.
climate Model; A climate model for projecting/forecasting measures like temperature, humidity etc.
Climate Model Experiment Type Enum; The specific type of climate model experiment, e.g. backcast (forecasts in the past) or future forecast of climate indicators, e.g. Temperature.
CMIP6 Historcial Run; CMIP6 model backcasts (forecasts for past dates) for climate variables, e.g. Temperature. These are also known as \historical experiments\ of the CMIP6 models.

Representative Concentration Pathway Enum; A Representative Concentration Pathway (RCP) is a greenhouse gas concentration (not emissions) trajectory adopted by the IPCC. The pathways describe different climate futures, all of which are considered possible depending on the volume of greenhouse gases (GHG) emitted in the years to come.
RCP1.9; A new pathway that focuses on limiting warming to below 1.5 C.
RCP2.6; Peak in radiative forcing at ~ 3 W/m2 before 2100 and decline.
RCP4.5; Stabilization without overshoot pathway to 4.5 W/m2 at stabilization after 2100.
RCP6.0; Stabilization without overshoot pathway to 6 W/m2 at stabilization after 2100.
RCP7.0; This is a baseline outcome rather than a mitigation target
RCP8.5; Rising radiative forcing pathway leading to 8.5 W/m2 in 2100.

Shared Socioeconomic Pathway Enum; Shared Socioeconomic Pathways (SSPs) are scenarios of projected socioeconomic global changes up to 2100. They are used to derive greenhouse gas emissions scenarios with different climate policies.

SSP1: Sustainability (Taking the Green Road); The world shifts gradually, but pervasively, toward a more sustainable path, emphasizing more inclusive development that respects perceived environmental boundaries.
SSP2: Middle of the Road; The world follows a path in which social, economic, and technological trends do not shift markedly from historical patterns.

SSP3: Regional rivalry (A Rocky Road); A resurgent nationalism, concerns about competitiveness and security, and regional conflicts push countries to increasingly focus on domestic or, at most, regional issues.

SSP5: Fossil-fueled Development; The world places increasing faith in competitive markets, innovation and participatory societies to produce rapid technological progress and development of human capital as the path to sustainable development.
wind Direction; Direction of the wind relative to the direction it originates
MP-Xylene; Refers to two isomers of Xylene, the m-Xylene and p-Xylene.
Shortwave Radiation; Radiant energy with wavelengths in the visible, near-ultraviolet, and near-infrared spectra.
Longwave Radiation; Electromagnetic radiation emitted by the surface and atmosphere at wavelengths between 3 and 100 μm, i.e. within the range of infrared radiation
Wind Component Enum; Represents the components of the wind vector.
Climate Model Enum; Type of climate model.
Ensemble across Climate Models; This model represents an ensemble across all available models

fema Social Vulnerability; The susceptibility of social groups to the adverse impacts of natural hazards, including disproportionate death, injury, loss, or disruption of livelihood. This is a measure defined and computed by the US Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA).

fema Community Resilience; The ability of a community to prepare for anticipated natural hazards, adapt to changing conditions, and withstand and recover rapidly from disruptions. This is a measure defined and computed by the US Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA).
fema Natural Hazard Risk Index; The potential for negative impacts as a result of a natural hazard. This is a measure defined and computed by the US Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA).
Natural Hazard Impact; Negative impacts, such as destruction or death, imposed upon factors such as Buildings, People, and Agriculture through occurrences of natural hazards.

heat Wave Intensity; Describes how hot the temperature is during heat waves. It is defined as the difference in temperature (measured in degrees Celsius) between the local threshold for heat waves and the average of the daily minimum temperature during the heat wave.
vapor Pressure Deficit; The difference (deficit) between the amount of moisture in the air and how much moisture the air can hold when it is saturated.
area Type; Identifies the type of land area, like forest, cropland, etc.
Natural Hazard Severity Enum; Describes the severity of a natural hazard
percent Deviation Value; Percentage change between actual and normal measurements.

hazard Type Risk Index Value; The FEMA National Risk Index (NRI) uses Hazard Type Risk Index values to represent the relative risk of a community for a specific natural hazard, compared to other communities. These values are calculated by combining Expected Annual Loss (EAL), Social Vulnerability, and Community Resilience data for each hazard type.

Initial Claim Excluding Intrastate Transitional; Used by State UI programs. Includes intrastate initial claims and interstate initial claims filed from the agent state, but not intrastate transitional claims.
Initial Claim; An initial request for unemployment benefits.
Continued Claim; A claim to continue receiving unemployment benefits.
State Unemployment Insurance; Unemployment insurance provided by a state (first-level administrative country subdivision) or state-equivalent.
UCFENo State Unemployment Insurance; Represents Unemployment Compensation for Federal Employees, not in conjunction with any State Unemployment Insurance program.
UCXOnly; Represents Unemployment Compensation for Ex-servicemembers, not in conjunction with any other unemployment insurance program.
Short Time Compensation; Short-Time Compensation (STC), also known as work sharing or shared-work program, is an alternative to layoffs for employers experiencing a reduction in available work.
State Unemployment Insurance Or Short Time Compensation; Represents any State Unemployment Insurance program or Short Time Compensation program.

dcid:With Covered Employment; Qualified for unemployment insurance benefits via conditions such as: earned a specified amount of wages or must have worked a certain number of weeks or calendar quarters in covered employment, or must have met some combination of the wage and employment requirements within his/her base period.

dcid:Moving Average1 Week Window; Moving average with a window of 1 week. A statistic attributed to any given day would be the average of that day plus the preceding 6 days. This does NOT make any statement about the frequency of observations.

dcid:Scaled For Part Time Unemployment Benefits; For Short-Time Compensation program observations. Indicates that the measurement of number of STC claims has been scaled down to the equivalent of the full time layoff initial claims that would have taken place if not for the STC program. That is, if the agreement is for a 20 percent reduction in each STC claimant's hours, then each of these claimants initial claim would represent 20 percent of an equivalent full time layoff initial claim.
COVID-19; Coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) is an infectious disease caused by severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-Co V-2).
Heat Stress; Exposure to extreme heat can result in occupational illnesses and injuries. Heat stress can result in heat stroke, heat exhaustion, heat cramps, or heat rashes.
Acute Condition; Acute illnesses generally develop suddenly and last a short time, often only a few days or weeks.
Chronic Condition; Chronic conditions develop slowly and may worsen over an extended period of time—months to years.

Perinatal Infection; Perinatal Infection, also referred as vertically transmitted infection is an infection caused by pathogens (such as bacteria and viruses) that use mother-to-child transmission, that is, transmission directly from the mother to an embryo, fetus, or baby during pregnancy or childbirth.
Invasive Disease; Invasive disease means that germs invade parts of the body that are normally free from germs.
Dementia; Dementia describe a group of symptoms affecting memory, thinking and social abilities severely enough to interfere with your daily life.
Kidney Disease; Kidney disease is a condition in which the kidneys are damaged and cannot filter blood as well as they should.
Lung Condition; Lung Condition is any problem in the lungs that prevents the lungs from working properly.

Imported Case; An imported case means that the patient was infected non-locally and imported the disease into the local area. This covers a wider range of importations, not only including cases imported from abroad.
Indigenous Case; An indigenous case means that the patient was infected locally and the disease is indigenous to the local area

Clinical Case; In epidemiology, a clinical case definition, a clinical definition, or simply a case definition lists the clinical criteria by which public health professionals determine whether a person's illness is included as a case in an outbreak investigation—that is, whether a person is considered directly affected by an outbreak
Pediatric Mortality; Mortality of patients admitted in Pediatric Intensive Care Unit (PICU)
Suspected Case; Clinically compatible cases without presumptive or confirmatory laboratory results
Underweight; A person having body mass index (BMI) smaller than 18.5.
Scheduled Caste; Scheduled Caste (SC) is an officially designated group of people in India.
Scheduled Tribe; Scheduled Tribe (ST) is an officially designated group of people in India.
Other Backward Class; Group of people in India who are not part of Scheduled Cast or Scheduled Tribe, but fall into backward classes.
General Category; Group of people in India who don't belong to any other categories.
Main Worker; Main workers are those who had worked for 6 months or more in the one year preceding the date of enumeration.
Marginal Worker; Marginal workers are those who had worked for less than 6 months in the one year preceding the date of enumeration.

Paid Worker; Includes people who worked for wages, salary, commission, tips, pay-in-kind, or piece rates for a private, for-profit employer or a private not-for-profit, tax-exempt or charitable organization
Incorporated Business Owner; A self-employed person who is an owner of an incorporated business, professional practice, or farm
Paid Employee; A salaried employee in the for-profit sector. Example: Salaried employee at a private company
Unincorporated Business Owner Or Unpaid Family Worker

Unincorporated Business Owner; People who worked for profit or fees in their own unincorporated business, profession, or trade, or who operated a farm. This class is often tabulated together with the following group, unpaid family workers.

Unpaid Family Worker; This includes people who worked without pay in a business or on a farm operated by a relative. Note that, on tabulations with earnings data, unpaid family workers may have earnings.
Homosexual; Of or relating to people who are physically, romantically, and/or emotionally attracted to people of the same sex.
Heterosexual; Of or relating to people who are physically, romantically, and/or emotionally attracted to people of the opposite sex.
Lesbian; Of or relating to women who are physically, romantically, and/or emotionally attracted to other women.
Gay; Of or relating to people who are physically, romantically, and/or emotionally attracted to people of the same sex (used especially for males).
Bisexual; Of or relating to people who are physically, romantically, and/or emotionally attracted to both men and women.
LGBT; Common initialism for 'lesbian, gay, bisexual, and transgender,'to refer to community organizations or events that serve lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, and allied people.

Offender Attributes Known; The term known offender does not imply the suspect’s identity is known; rather, the term indicates some aspect of the suspect was identified, thus distinguishing the suspect from an unknown offender.
Local Currency; The actual currency of a region used during a particular observation period.
Standardized Currency; The standardized currency used by a region in its most recent reference year.
SGP_Other Ethnicity; Persons of an ethnicity not explicitly listed in the Singapore Census
Malay; Malay is an Austronesian ethnic group native to eastern Sumatra, the Malay Peninsula and coastal Borneo

Assessment Level 1; The lowest level of performance in an assessment, often indicating that the student has not met the achievement standard or demonstrates a minimal understanding of the subject matter.

Assessment Level 2; The second level of performance in an assessment, typically indicating that the student nearly met the achievement standard or demonstrates a partial understanding of the subject matter.

Assessment Level 3; The third level of performance in an assessment, generally indicating that the student met the achievement standard or demonstrates a proficient understanding of the subject matter.
Assessment Level 4; The highest level of performance in an assessment, indicating that the student exceeded the achievement standard or demonstrates an advanced understanding of the subject matter.
Assessment Level 3 And Level 4; A combined performance category representing students who either met or exceeded the achievement standard (Proficient or Advanced).
type Of; Indicates the type of an entity. The type should be the Class of which the entity is an instance. For example, Addis Ababa is a type Of City

sub Class Of; Indicates one or more superclass(es) of this class, in a class hierarchy. For example, City is a sub Class Of Administrative Area. All instances of the entity inherit the superclass. Thus, instances of City are also instances of Administrative Area.
sub Property Of; Indicates the parent of this property, in a property hierarchy.
equivalent Class; Provenance URL of an equivalent Class in another ontology (e.g. DCMI).
equivalent Property; Provenance URL of an equivalent Property in another ontology (e.g. DCMI).
description Url; The URL that provides a description used in the 'description' field of a Node.
name With Language; Text string in quotes ending with @<lang> where <lang> is a 2-letter ISO 639-1 language code.
geo Id; An identifier for a place, usually assigned by an administrative agency. For example, Miami-Dade county has a FIPS ID 12086 given by the US government.
us Census Geo Id; A geographic identifier that uniquely identifies all administrative/legal and statistical geographic areas for which the U.S. Census Bureau tabulates data.
margin Of Error; A statistic expressing the amount of random sampling error in the results of a survey.
margin Of Error Significance Level; The probability (expressed as a percentage) of a predicted/estimated value falling within the margin of error (absolute error) specified.

std Error; The standard error of a statistic (usually an estimate of a parameter) is the standard deviation of its sampling distribution or an estimate of that standard deviation. If the parameter or the statistic is the mean, it is called the standard error of the mean.
unit; The unit of measurement used in an observation or statistical variable. For example, it could be USDollars, Meter, Celsius, etc. Values are typically of type Unit Of Measure.
Cohort; Group of people banded together or taken as a group.
mean Value; A value for the stat Type property of a Statistical Variable. Indicates that the variable reports the mean of a range of observations. An example is mean wind speed.
Election; A formal group decision-making process by which a population chooses an individual to hold public office.

General Election; A general election is a political voting election where generally all or most members of a given political body are chosen. These are usually held for a nation, state, or territory's primary legislative body, and are different from by-elections (only one electorate goes to election).
Presidential Election; A presidential election is the election of any head of state whose official title is President.
measurement Method; Provides additional information about how the measured Property of a Statistical Variable was measured.
measurement Result; A value of the stat Type property of a Statistical Variable. Indicates that the variable represents the result of some calculation or answer to a research hypothesis.

growth Rate; A value of the stat Type property of a Statistical Variable. Indicates that the variable measures the percentage change in observations over a certain time period. An example of this is a price index.
percentile10; A value of the stat Type property of a Statistical Variable. Indicates that the variable measures the 10th percentile of some population.
percentile25; A value of the stat Type property of a Statistical Variable. Indicates that the variable measures the 25th percentile of some population.
percentile75; A value of the stat Type property of a Statistical Variable. Indicates that the variable measures the 75th percentile of some population.
percentile90; A value of the stat Type property of a Statistical Variable. Indicates that the variable measures the 90th percentile of some population.
Prefecture; Administrative jurisdiction, regional or local government subdivision in various countries
Eurostat NUTS1; First level statistical subdivision within an EU member country
Eurostat NUTS2; Second level statistical subdivision within an EU member country
Eurostat NUTS3; Third level statistical subdivision within an EU member country
Administrative Area1; A level-1 administrative subdivision of a country, e.g. departments in Bolivia.
Administrative Area2; A level-2 administrative subdivision of a country, e.g. municipalities in Bulgaria.
Administrative Area3; A level-3 administrative subdivision of a country, e.g. municipalities in Bosnia and Herzegovina.
Administrative Area4; A level-4 administrative subdivision of a country, e.g. wards in South Africa.
Administrative Area5; A level-5 administrative subdivision of a country.
Town; A settlement that is bigger than a village but smaller than a city.
Village; A small clustered human settlement smaller than a town.
Neighborhood; A geographically localized community within a larger city, town or suburb.
USMetropolitan Division; Subdivisions of US Metropolitan Statistical Areas (MSAs) and New England City and Town Areas (NECTAs).
Census Output Area; Output Areas (OA) are the lowest level of geographical area for census statistics in the United Kingdom.

Lower Layer Super Output Area; Lower layer Super Output Areas (LSOA) are geographical area for census statistics in the United kingdom. They are made up of groups of Output Area (OAs), usually four or five. They comprise between 400 and 1,200 households and have a usually resident population between 1,000 and 3,000 persons.

Middle Layer Super Output Area; Middle layer Super Output Areas (MSOA) are geographical area for census statistics in the United kingdom. They are made up of groups of Lower layer Super Output Area (LSOAs), usually four or five. They comprise between 2,000 and 6,000 households and have a usually resident population between 5,000 and 15,000 persons. MSOAs fit within local authorities.
Electoral Ward; Electoral wards are spatial units of UK administrative geography used to elect local government councillors.

government Statistical Service Code; Government Statistical Service (GSS) codes are nine-character geocodes maintained by the United Kingdom's Office for National Statistics to represent a wide range of geographical areas of the UK, for use in tabulating census and other statistical data. The first three characters of the GSS code indicate the level of geography, and the six digits following define the individual unit.
Pin Code Area; Postal delivery area representations of India Post.
pin Code; PIN code is a six digit code in Indian postal code system used by India Post.
administrative Capital; The municipality exercising primary status in a country, state, province, or other administrative region, usually as its seat of government.
unweighted Sample Count Of The Population

Project; An enterprise (potentially individual but typically collaborative), planned to achieve a particular aim. Use properties from Organization, sub Organization/parent Organization to indicate project sub-structures
observation Period; The frequency with which an observation is reported. The format follows `P[0-9]*[Y|M|D|h|m|s]`. For example, P1 Y is Period 1 Year, P3 M is Period 3 Months, P3h is Period 3 hours.

accumulation Period; The length of time over which an observation was recorded. For example, an observation could have an observation Period of P1 Y (reported annually), but have an accumulation Period of P1 M (recorded monthly)
dcid; The unique Data Commons identifier for an entity or property.
kml Coordinates; Geographical boundary of a place in Keyhole Markup Language (KML).
geo Json Coordinates; Geographical boundary of a place in Geo JSON format.
geo Json Coordinates DP1; Simplified geographical boundary of a place in Geo JSON format. Created by running the Ramer-Douglas-Peucker algorithm on geo Json Coordinates with epsilon of 0.01.
geo Json Coordinates DP2; Simplified geographical boundary of a place in Geo JSON format. Created by running the Ramer-Douglas-Peucker algorithm on geo Json Coordinates with epsilon of 0.03.
geo Json Coordinates DP3; Simplified geographical boundary of a place in Geo JSON format. Created by running the Ramer-Douglas-Peucker algorithm on geo Json Coordinates with epsilon of 0.05.
geo Json Coordinates UN; Geographical boundary of a place in Geo JSON format from United Nations.
geo Json Coordinates UNDP1; Simplified geographical boundary of a place in Geo JSON format. Created by running the Ramer-Douglas-Peucker algorithm on geo Json Coordinates with epsilon of 0.01.
geo Json Coordinates UNDP2; Simplified geographical boundary of a place in Geo JSON format. Created by running the Ramer-Douglas-Peucker algorithm on geo Json Coordinates with epsilon of 0.03.
geo Json Coordinates UNDP3; Simplified geographical boundary of a place in Geo JSON format. Created by running the Ramer-Douglas-Peucker algorithm on geo Json Coordinates with epsilon of 0.05.
geo Json Coordinates India; Geographical boundary of a place in Geo JSON format with official Indian boundary.

geo Json Coordinates India DP1; Simplified geographical boundary of a place in Geo JSON format. Created by running the Ramer-Douglas-Peucker algorithm on geo Json Coordinates India with epsilon of 0.01.

geo Json Coordinates India DP2; Simplified geographical boundary of a place in Geo JSON format. Created by running the Ramer-Douglas-Peucker algorithm on geo Json Coordinates India with epsilon of 0.03.

geo Json Coordinates India DP3; Simplified geographical boundary of a place in Geo JSON format. Created by running the Ramer-Douglas-Peucker algorithm on geo Json Coordinates India with epsilon of 0.05.
Criminal Activities; Refers to an offense which is a breach of the criminal laws.
Criminal Incidents; A criminal incident is defined as one or more offenses committed by the same offender, or group of offenders acting in concert, at the same time and place.

Hate Crime Incidents; A hate crime is a criminal offense against a person or property motivated in whole or in part by an offender's bias against a race, religion, disability, sexual orientation, ethnicity, gender, or gender identity.
location; Indicates the location at which an event occurred, often reported as geographical coordinates.
min Value; A value of the stat Type property of a Statistical Variable. Indicates that the variable reports the minimum value of a range of observations. An example is minimum temperature.
wikidata Id; The unique Wikidata identifier for an entity or property.

sexual Orientation; The term for a person's physical, romantic, and/or emotional attraction to members of the same and/or opposite sex, including lesbian, gay, bisexual, and heterosexual (straight) individuals.
max Value; A value of the stat Type property of a Statistical Variable. Indicates that the variable reports the maximum value of a range of observations. An example is maximum temperature.

value; The recorded value of a Stat Var Observation. Also used as a value of the measured Property property of a Statistical Variable to indicate that all observations for that variable provide the actual recorded value.

median Value; A value of the stat Type property of a Statistical Variable. Indicates that the variable reports the median value of a range of observations. An example is the median age of a population.

std Deviation Value; A value of the stat Type property of a Statistical Variable. Indicates that the variable reports the standard deviation over a range of observations. An example is the standard deviation from the average test score of a population.

skewness Value; A value of the stat Type property of a Statistical Variable. Indicates that the variable reports the skewness over a range of observations. An example is the skewness value of a range of temperature measurements.

kurtosis Value; A value of the stat Type property of a Statistical Variable. Indicates that the variable reports the kurtosis over a range of observations. An example is the kurtosis value of a range of temperature measurements.
decile Value; A value of the stat Type property of a Statistical Variable. Indicates that the variable reports the value of one of the 10 deciles of a range of observations.
ranking Value; A value of the stat Type property of a Statistical Variable. Indicates that the variable reports the relative ranking of a sorted range of observations.

sample Size; A value of the stat Type property of a Statistical Variable. Indicates that the variable reports the sample size used to derive a set of observations. For example, to calculate the percentage of a population, results may be extrapolated from a sample of observations.
count; A value of the measured Property property of a Statistical Variable. Indicates that the variable reports a count of the measured population.
cumulative Count; A value of the measured Property property of a Statistical Variable. Indicates that the variable reports the running count of all instances of the measured population.

incremental Count; A value of the measured Property property of a Statistical Variable. Indicates that the variable reports only the incremental count for the current instances of the measured population
days Since Last Reported Case; Number of days since the last reported case.
Election Campaign; An effort led by candidates of elections in order to be elected to hold public office like that of House of Representatives, President, etc
bea Product; Products and Services designated by US Bureau of Economic Analysis, of consumer spending (such as food, clothing, shelter, and services) that people buy for day-to-day living
gross Domestic Product; Gross domestic product (GDP) is the monetary value of all the finished goods and services produced within a place in a specific time period

USCEstablishment; An establishment or business as defined by US Census Bureau, which is ata fixed physical location or permanent structure, where some form of business activity is conducted. A single firm or a company operating in multiple locations can have many establishments

USCFirm; A firm is typically an organizational entity, comparable to the familiar use of the word company. A firm can exist in one location or have many locations and can have multiple establishments.

USCWorker; An employee or worker as defined by US Census Bureau that excludes self-employed individuals, employees of private households, railroad employees, agricultural production employees and most government employees
child Birth Order; The birth order of the child.A first-order birth is the first child born to parents, second-order is the second, and so on.

Biological Specimen; Biological Specimens facilitate the sharing of information about biological diversity by providing identifiers, labels, and definitions. The class definition is loosely based on the Darwin Core schema.
mothers Age; The age of the mother at the time of birth
mothers Race; The race of the mother
mothers Education; The highest level of education attained by the mother at the time of birth
mothers Ethnicity; The ethnicity of the mother
mothers Nativity; The nativity of the mother

Statistical Aggregation; Custom aggregation of statistical data in Data Commons, computed by Data Commons. For example, aggregating population with health insurance stats available at county-level to state-level.

Aggregation Slice; Filter to a Data Commons Statistical Aggregation consisting of a property and one or more values, which will further slice the output data. For example, the count of earthquakes might be sliced by different magnitude ranges.

Statistical Calculation; Custom calculation in Data Commons, computed by Data Commons. For example, computing the population without health insurance using an exhaustive age breakdown of such population.
Property being aggregated in a Data Commons Statistical Aggregation. This could be a Statistical Variable when aggregating across places, or a constraint property when aggregating entities.
Method by which data is aggregated (minimum, maximum, sum, etc).
Filter to a Data Commons Statistical Aggregation, which will further slice the output data. Output Statistical Variables will be generated for each slice in the filter.
Property to filter a Data Commons Statistical Aggregation by. Entities will be grouped by this property when computing the aggregation.
Value to filter a Data Commons Statistical Aggregation by. Entities will be grouped by this value when computing the aggregation.
Type of input entity to a Data Commons Statistical Aggregation (place type, event type, etc).

Input property in a Data Commons Statistical Aggregation that will be used to indicate the time. Required for aggregating entities. Output Stat Var Observations will use this property to determine their date.
Property linking input to output entities in a Data Commons Statistical Aggregation, such as containment, membership, or hierarchy.
Type of output entity to a Data Commons Statistical Aggregation. The output Stat Var Observations will be attached to entities associated with this type.
Date pattern (ISO 8601) to match for aggregating by time. Inputs will by grouped by this date pattern, and the output Stat Var Observations will have dates of this pattern.

Mathematical expression consisting of properties (typically Statistical Variables) representing a Data Commons Statistical Calculation. Valid operators include +, -, *, /. For example, (Count_Person_Male + Count_Person_Female).
Type of entity for which to perform a Data Commons Statistical Calculation.
Output property to hold the result of a Data Commons Statistical Calculation. This is typically a Statistical Variable.

dcid:public School Type; School Type is a classification of schools conducting elementary and/or secondary instruction programs according to the ordinary or special instructional needs of students. Four categories of schools are: Regular School: A public elementary/secondary school that does not focus primarily on vocational, special, or alternative education. Special Education School: Public elementary/secondary school that focuses primarily on education of one or more of the following types of students: hard of hearing, deaf, speech-impaired, health-impaired, orthopedically impaired, intellectual disability, seriously emotionally disturbed, multi-handicapped, visually handicapped, or deaf and blind, and adapts curriculum, materials or instructions for students served. Vocational School: Public elementary/secondary school that focuses primarily on vocational education, and provides education in one or more semi-skilled technical operations. Other/Alternative School: A public elementary/secondary school that addresses the needs of students which typically cannot be met in a regular school, provides nontraditional education, serves as an adjunct to a regular school, and falls outside of the categories of regular, special education, or vocational education.

dcid:National School Lunch Program Enum; This variable indicates whether a school participates in the National School Lunch Program, and, if so, under what special provisions. The Healthy, Hunger-Free Kids Act of 2010 (PL 111-296) includes provisions for determining free and reduced-price lunch (FRL) eligibility that may affect the reliability and availability of the FRL counts reported to EDFacts (FS033). Under provisions 2 and 3 of the law, annual certification of individual students is not required. A new provision, the Community Eligibility Option (CEO or Provision 4), eliminates the requirement for individual eligibility information once a school has determined a baseline percentage of FRL eligible students. These changes may result in missing or out of date FRL counts. Education researchers frequently use FRL eligibility as an indicator of student socioeconomic status (SES). The NSLP status provides these researchers with an indication of the reliability of the FRL counts reported in this file.

crsi Score; The Natural Hazard Resilience Screening Index (Na HRSI; previously entitled Climate Resilience Screening Index [CRSI]) was developed as an endpoint for characterizing county resilience outcomes that are based on risk profiles and responsive to changes in governance, societal, built, and natural system characteristics.

Superfund Funding Status Enum; Superfund sites can be categorized into two classes (i) In the National Priority List (NPL) site and (ii) Non-NPL site. The other means through which clean-up operations at sites are financed include (i)individuals or companies responsible for the problems can clean up voluntarily with EPA or State Supervision. (ii) The responsible party or parties can be ordered to clean up by Federal or State legal action. (iii)A State or local government can choose to assume the responsibility to clean up without Federal dollars. (iv)The Superfund Trust Fund can pay for the cleanup, then seek to recover the costs later from the responsible party or parties. A site needs to be on the NPL in order for it to be eligible to financed through the Superfund Trust Fund.

NPLSite; NPL or National Priority List contains superfund sites that need further investigations to determine whether the sites represent a long-term threat to public health or the environment and, therefore, need remedial action (clean-up). NPL sites are further classified based on the site's status on the NPL list. Thus, an NPL Site can be (i)Proposed NPL Site, (ii) Final NPL Site [site is in the NPL list] and (iii) Deleted NPL site [site is removed from the NPL list].

Not NPLSite; Non-NPL sites include sites removed from the proposed NPL, sites withdrawn from the final NPL, sites being addressed as part of another NPL site, and all other non-NPL sites. Where available, the report includes funding information for each action, as well as site characterization data.
Proposed NPLSite; A site is proposed to be part of the National Priority List
Final NPLSite; A site that has been proposed to the National Priorities List is \"final\" when it has been formally added to the National Priorities List (NPL).
Deleted NPLSite; EPA may delete a final NPL site if it determines that no further response is required to protect human health or the environment.

maturity; The length of time until the maturity date, on which the issuer is obligated to repay the nominal amount
Bond; In finance, a bond is an instrument of indebtedness of the bond issuer to the holders.

Treasury Security; United States Treasury securities are government debt instruments issued by the United States Department of the Treasury to finance government spending as an alternative to taxation.

Treasury Bill; A Treasury Bill is a zero-coupon Treasury security that matures in one year or less. Treasury Bills are bought at a discount of the par value and eventually redeemed at that par value to create a positive yield to maturity.
Treasury Note; A Treasury Note is a Treasury security with a maturity of 2, 3, 5, 7, or 10 years, and a coupon payment every six months.
Treasury Bond; A Treasury Bond is a Treasury security with a maturity of thirty years, and a coupon payment every six months.

flood Zone Type; Flood zone describing the risk level or likelihood of floods. The US Federal Emergency management Agency (FEMA) publishes a Flood Insurance Rate Map (FIRM) with flood risk for specific regions that is used to rate the insured property.
Insured Item Enum; The specific type of items covered under an insurance policy.
FEMAFlood Zone Enum; The land areas that could be covered by flood waters.

High Risk Zone (100 year flood); Special Flood Hazard Areas (SFHA) that have a 1-percent chance of annual floods also referred to as 100-year flood. They are shown on the flood maps as zones labeled as Zone A, Zone AO, Zone AH, Zones A1-A30, Zone AE, Zone A99, Zone AR, Zone AR/AE, Zone AR/AO, Zone AR/A1-A30, Zone AR/A, Zone V, Zone VE, and Zones V1-V30.
Moderate Risk Zone (500 year flood); In moderate risk areas have a 0.2 percent chance of annual floods also referred to as 500-year flood. These flood zones are labelled as Zone B or Zone X(shaded).
Low Risk Zone (500 year flood); In low risk areas have a lower than 0.2 percent chance of annual floods. These flood zones are labelled as Zone C or Zone X(unshaded).

Undetermined Risk Zone; No flood-hazard analysis has been conducted in these areas, but a flood risk still exists. Flood insurance rates reflect the uncertainty of the flood risk. These areas are labeled with the letter D on the flood maps.

Zone A; Areas subject to inundation by the 1-percent-annual-chance flood event generally determined using approximate methodologies. Flood risk determined by detailed methods with base flood elevations are designated AE or A1-30.
Zone A99; Areas subject to inundation by the 1-percent-annual-chance flood event, but which will ultimately be protected upon completion of an under-construction Federal flood protection system.
Zone AH; Areas subject to inundation by 1-percent-annual-chance shallow flooding (usually areas of ponding) where average depths are between one and three feet.
Zone AO; Areas subject to inundation by 1-percent-annual-chance shallow flooding (usually sheet flow on sloping terrain) where average depths are between one and three feet.
Zone AR; Areas that result from the decertification of a previously accredited flood protection system that is determined to be in the process of being restored to provide base flood protection.
Zone V; Areas along coasts subject to inundation by the 1-percent-annual-chance flood event with additional hazards associated with storm-induced waves.
Zone X; An area that is outside the Special Flood hazard Area with moderate or minimal flood hazard.

Incarcerated; Indicates the state of being confined in a prison or a jail. This may also include halfway houses, boot camps, weekend programs, and other facilities in which individuals are locked up overnight.
Prison Sentence Status Enum; An enumeration of prison sentence statuses.
Unsentenced; Indicates state of not having received a prison sentence.
Sentenced; Indicates state of having received a prison sentence.
Prisoner Measurement Basis Enum; Enumeration for the basis of measurement of the prisoner statistic.
Measured Based On Custody; Measurement based on those in custody of state or federal correctional officials.

Measured Based On Jurisdiction; Measurement based on those under the jurisdiction of the state meaning all state and federal inmates held in a public or private prison and those held in jail facilities either physically located inside or outside of the state of legal responsibility, and other inmates who may be temporarily out to court or in transit from the jurisdiction of legal authority to the custody of a confinement facility outside that jurisdiction.
Cause Of Death Enum; Enumeration for causes of death.
Judicial Execution; Execution for committing a crime.
Illness Or Natural Cause; Illness or other natural cause of death excluding AIDS.
NPSOther Cause Of Death; Other cause of death not already listed.
correctional Facility Operator; The level that the correctional facility is operated at.
Correctional Facility Operator Enum; Enumeration of the different levels correctional facilities are operated at.
correctional Facility Location; Where the correctional facility is located relative to a location.
Correctional Facility Location Enum; Enumeration of relative locations of correctional facilities.
In State; Correctional facility located within the state.
Out Of State; Correctional facility located outside of the state.
Incarceration Event; An event that affects one's state of being incarcerated.
event Type; Additional information about the type of an event.
Incarceration Event Type Enum; Enumeration of types of incarceration events.
Oceanic Basin; In hydrology, an oceanic basin may be anywhere on Earth that is covered by seawater but geologically ocean basins are large geologic basins that are below sea level.
Meteorological Event; Meteorological or atmospheric events include lightning, flash floods, etc; violent meteorological events are commonly known as storms.
Cyclone Event; A cyclone is a type of storm with a large scale air mass that rotates around a strong center of low atmospheric pressure.

Wildland Fire Event; Wildland fire is any non-structure fire that occurs in an area in which development is essentially non-existent, except for roads, railroads, power lines, and similar transportation facilities.
Prescribed Fire Event; A wildland fire originating from a planned ignition in accordance with applicable laws, policies, and regulations to meet specific objectives.

Fire Incident Complex Event; Two or more distinct incidents in the same general area that, by management action, are managed under a single incident commander or unified command in order to improve efficiency and simplify incident management processes.
Fire Event; An occurrence of a fire that burns through wildland, building, dwelling or other structure.  Often involves the use of fire department resources to contain it.
Storm Episode; A continuous disturbance of the atmosphere marked by wind and usually by rain, snow, hail, sleet, or thunder and lightning.
Storm Event; A singular event of rain, snow, hail, sleet, etc. within a storm episode.
Cold Wave Event; Occurrence of a cold wave event.
Heat Wave Event; Occurrence of a heat wave event.
Wet Bulb Temperature Event; A wet-bulb event happens when the estimated wet-bulb temperature (function of temperature and humidity) exceeds a threshold in Celsius.
Wet Bulb Temperature Celsius31 Event; A wet-bulb event which happens when the wet bulb temperature exceeds 31 degrees Celsius.
Wet Bulb Temperature Celsius32 Event; A wet-bulb event which happens when the wet bulb temperature exceeds 32 degrees Celsius.
Wet Bulb Temperature Celsius33 Event; A wet-bulb event which happens when the wet bulb temperature exceeds 33 degrees Celsius.
Wet Bulb Temperature Celsius34 Event; A wet-bulb event which happens when the wet bulb temperature exceeds 34 degrees Celsius.
Wet Bulb Temperature Celsius35 Event; A wet-bulb event which happens when the wet bulb temperature exceeds 35 degrees Celsius.
Wet Bulb Temperature Celsius36 Event; A wet-bulb event which happens when the wet bulb temperature exceeds 36 degrees Celsius.

Temperature Event; A temperature event happens when the max/min temperature for a period (e.g. over a day) exceeds or is less than the reference (e.g. a long term average for the month) by a certain amount.

Heat Temperature Event; A heat (temperature) event happens when the maximum temperature for a period (e.g. over a day) exceeds the reference (e.g. a long term average for the month) by a certain amount.

Cold Temperature Event; A cold (temperature) event happens when the minimum temperature for a period (e.g. over a day) is below the reference (e.g. a long term average for the month) by a certain amount.
Riverine Flooding Event; Occurrence of a riverine flooding event.
Volcanic Activity Event; Occurrence of a volcanic activity event.
Cyclone Type Enum; Different classifications of cyclones.

Tropical Cyclone; A warm-core non-frontal synoptic-scale cyclone, originating over tropical or subtropical waters, with organized deep convection and a closed surface wind circulation about a well-defined low-pressure center. Once formed, a tropical cyclone is maintained by the extraction of heat energy from the ocean at high temperature and heat export at the low temperatures of the upper troposphere.
Extratropical Cyclone; A cyclone of any intensity for which the primary energy source is baro clinic, that is, results from the temperature contrast between warm and cold air masses.
Subtropical Cyclone; A non-frontal low-pressure system that has characteristics of both tropical and extra tropical cyclones.
Remnant Low; A low-pressure system that is neither a tropical cyclone, a subtropical cyclone, nor an extratropical cyclone (of any intensity).
Tropical Wave; A trough or cyclonic curvature maximum in the trade-wind easterlies (of any intensity). The wave may reach maximum amplitude in the lower middle troposphere.

Tropical Disturbance; A discrete tropical weather system of apparently organized convection -- generally 100 to 300 nmi in diameter -- originating in the tropics or subtropics, having a non frontal migratory character, and maintaining its identity for 24 hours or more. It may or may not be associated with a detectable perturbation of the wind field.
Tornado Intensity Enum; Classifications of tornado intensity.
Tornado Intensity EF0; Enhanced Fujita Scale classification EF0 – Light Damage (40 – 72 mph)
Tornado Intensity EF1; Enhanced Fujita Scale classification EF1 – Moderate Damage (73 – 112 mph)
Tornado Intensity EF2; Enhanced Fujita Scale classification EF2 – Significant Damage (113 – 157 mph)
Tornado Intensity EF3; Enhanced Fujita Scale classification EF3 – Severe Damage (158 – 206 mph)
Tornado Intensity EF4; Enhanced Fujita Scale classification EF4 – Devastating Damage (207 – 260 mph)
Tornado Intensity EF5; Enhanced Fujita Scale classification EF5 – Incredible Damage (261 – 318 mph)
Tornado Intensity EFU; Enhanced Fujita Scale classification EF Unknown – Tornado Without Visible Damage Evidence
Tornado Intensity F0; Original Fujita Scale classification F0 – Light Damage (estimated 40 - 72 mph)
Tornado Intensity F1; Original Fujita Scale classification F1 – Moderate Damage (estimated 73 - 112 mph)
Tornado Intensity F2; Original Fujita Scale classification F2 – Considerable Damage (estimated 113 - 157 mph)
Tornado Intensity F3; Original Fujita Scale classification F3 – Severe Damage (estimated 158 - 206 mph)
Tornado Intensity F4; Original Fujita Scale classification F4 – Devastating Damage (estimated 207 - 260 mph)
Tornado Intensity F5; Original Fujita Scale classification F5 – Incredible Damage (estimated 261 - 318 mph)
Wind Speed Magnitude Type Enum; Types of wind speed magnitude measurements.
Precipitation Type Enum; Types of precipitation. E.g. hail, snow, rain.
Flood Cause Enum; Possible flood causes.
classification; A variable classification of a thing.
max Wind Speed; Maximum recorded or estimated wind speed.
min Pressure; Minimum recorded or estimated pressure.
max Classification; Maximum classification level of a Meteorological Event.
precipitation Accumulation; The amount of precipitation accumulated over the course of a meteorological event.
precipitation Type; Precipitation type is the diagnosed form of precipitation reaching the surface, based on atmospheric temperature structure and hydrometeor characteristics.
length Traveled; Measure of the path length traveled by a meteorological phenomenon, such as a tornado.
distance To Land; Distance to land from the center of cyclone.
cyclone Track Type; Type of cyclone track record.
IBTr ACS_Cyclone Track Type Enum; Types of cyclone track records used by IBTr ACS.
Main Cyclone Track; Primary track associated with a storm system. This is a track that has had some reanalysis and is higher quality than provisional data.

Provisional Cyclone Track; Real time data used to populate the position and other parameters of this system. This is a provisional track that will be replaced when reanalysis of the storm is performed (usually within 2 years of the storm's occurrence).

Provisional Spur Cyclone Track; Real time data (see Provisional Cyclone Track) but due to differences in positions between various inputs, algorithm can not identify accurate position. When counting storms, these should not likely be counted. These should be rare for PROVISIONAL data.

Spur Cyclone Track; Usually short lived tracks associated with a main track and often represents alternate positions at the beginning of a system. Can also represent actual system interactions (e.g., Fujiwhara interactions).
Fire Incident Type Enum; Types of incidents and planned events to which the interagency wildland fire community responds.

Wildfire; An unplanned, unwanted wildland fire including unauthorized human-caused fires, escaped wildland fire use events, escaped prescribed fire projects, and all other wildland fires where the objective is to put the fire out (NIFC.gov).

Wildland Fire Use; The application of the appropriate management response to naturally ignited wildland fires to accomplish specific resource management objectives in pre-defined designated areas outlined in Fire Management Plans (NIFC.gov).

Prescribed Fire; Any fire ignited by management actions to meet specific objectives, e.g., the establishment/maintenance of healthy forests and grasslands or the enhancement of certain wildlife habitat. Prescribed fire is a well-established practice on public and private lands throughout the world, and is based on years of scientific research. Resource managers carefully calculate meteorological factors, fuels, slope of land, and other relevant conditions in planning prescribed fires. Utmost care is taken to protect human life and property, manage impacts of smoke, protect historical and archeological resources, and protect the ecological integrity of the physical and biological resources (NIFC.gov).
Fire Cause Enum; Possible fire causes.
Fire Cause General Enum; General fire causes.
Fire Cause Specific Enum; Specific fire causes.

discovery Date; The date the fire was confirmed to exist or was reported as discovered.

controlled Date; When the fire was declared controlled, where controlled is defined as: The completion of control line around a fire, any spot fires therefrom, and any interior islands to be saved; burned out any unburned area adjacent to the fire side of control lines; and cool down all hot spots that are immediate threats to the control line, until the lines can be reasonably expected to hold under the foreseeable conditions.
expected Loss; The estimated economic loss resulting from natural hazards. It quantifies loss for relevant consequence types (buildings, people, and agriculture), and in aggregate.
containment Date; The date and time a wildfire was declared contained.
initial Response Date; The date/time of the initial response to the incident.

burned Area; A measure of acres reported for a fire.  More specifically, the number of acres within the current perimeter of a specific, individual incident, including unburned and unburnable islands.
initial Response Area; An estimate of acres burning at the time of initial response. More specifically when the IC arrives and performs initial size up.
total Incident Personnel; The total number of personnel assigned. Includes overhead, crew members, helicopter crew member, engine crew members, camp crew people, etc.

wfigs Fire ID; Unique identifier assigned to each wildland fire.  yyyy = calendar year, SSUUUU = POO protecting unit identifier (5 or 6 characters), xxxxxx = local incident identifier (6 to 10 characters)
irwin ID; Unique identifier assigned to each incident record in IRWIN(Integrated Reporting of Wildland-Fire Information).
incident Location Description; General descriptive location of the incident such as the number of miles from an identifiable town.
fire Perimeter; Geographic coordinates indicating the spread of fire.
cyclone Type; Classification of a cyclone.
pressure; Force applied perpendicular to the surface of an object per unit area over which that force is distributed.
mixing Ratio; Mass of water vapor per unit mass of dry air.
reflectivity; Amount of radar energy that is returned (reflected) back toward a radar after hitting targets in the atmosphere
composite Reflectivity; A radar product that is composed of the greatest echo intensity (reflectivity) from any elevation angle seen by the radar.
Planetary Boundary Layer; Lowest part of the atmosphere directly influenced by its contact with a planetary surface.
ventilation Rate; Product of the mixing height multiplied by the transport wind speed.
geopotential Height; It measures the height of a pressure surface above sea level, accounting for the fact that gravity varies slightly across the Earth.
velocity; Rate of change of position with respect to time including magnitude and direction.
Pressure Vertical Velocity; Vertical motion of air expressed as rate of pressure change with time
Geometric Vertical Velocity; Vertical component of air motion expressed in height (geometric) coordinates, representing the rate of change of an air parcel's physical height with time.
absolute Vorticity; Measure of the total rotation of an air parcel in the atmosphere

haines Index; A meteorological fire-weather index designed to evaluate the potential for large or erratic wildfire behavior due to atmospheric conditions, specifically dryness and instability in the lower atmosphere.

Mean Sea Level Pressure ETA Model Reduction; A specialized, computationally derived technique for calculating atmospheric pressure at sea level, specifically designed to address the unique step-mountain topography of the Eta model to prevent spurious, unrealistic pressure patterns in mountainous regions.
volumetric Soil Moisture; Water contained in the upper part of the soil mantle. This moisture evaporates from the soil and is used and transpired by vegetation.
liquid Water Content; Liquid water content is the mass of liquid water droplets contained in a unit volume of air.
cloud Water Content; Cloud water content is the mass of liquid cloud droplets suspended in air, excluding precipitation-sized water drops.
snow Water Equivalent; The water content obtained from melting accumulated snow.
thickness; Thickness is the measurement of the distance (in meters) between any two constant pressure surfaces.
Apparent; A measure of human discomfort due to combined heat and humidity (e.g., heat index).
frozen Precipitation; Frozen precipitation is precipitation that falls to the surface in solid form, such as snow or ice pellets.
Ice pellets; pellets of ice composed of frozen or mostly frozen raindrops or refrozen partially melted snowflakes
surface Roughness; Parameter describing the aerodynamic roughness of the Earth's surface that influences near-surface wind and surface-atmosphere momentum exchange.
frictional Velocity; A turbulence velocity scale derived from surface stress that characterizes momentum transfer between the atmosphere and the surface.
soil Type; Classification of soil based on physical and hydraulic properties.
Soil Type Enum; An enumeration of different soil types.
wilting Point; The minimum amount of water in the soil that the plant requires not to wilt.
field Capacity; The soil moisture content remaining after excess water has drained and is available for plant uptake.
sunshine Duration; The amount of time sunlight was detected at a given point.
surface Lifted Index; Difference between the observed 500-h Pa temperature and the temperature of a surface-based air parcel lifted to 500 h Pa.
convective Available Potential Energy; A measure of the amount of energy available for convection
convective Inhibition; The integrated negative buoyancy that must be overcome for an air parcel to reach its level of free convection.
cloud Water; The liquid water content of clouds in the form of suspended cloud droplets.

Middle Cloud Layer; A term used to signify clouds with bases between 6,500 and 23,000 feet. At the higher altitudes, they may also have some ice crystals, but they are composed mainly of water droplets.

High Cloud Layer; These clouds have bases between 16,500 and 45,000 feet in the mid latitudes. At this level they are composed of primarily of ice crystals. Some clouds at this level are cirrus, cirrocumulus, and cirrostratus.
Cloud Ceiling; The height above ground level of the lowest cloud layer covering more than half of the sky, diagnosed from model cloud fields.
storm Relative Helicity; The integrated measure of streamwise vorticity in the lower atmosphere relative to storm motion, indicating the potential for rotating updrafts.
storm Motion; The speed and direction at which a thunderstorm travels.

Tropopause; The upper boundary of the troposphere, usually characterized by an abrupt change in lapse rate from positive (decreasing temperature with height) to neutral or negative (temperature constant or increasing with height).
altitude; Altitude is a distance measurement, usually in the vertical or up direction, between a reference datum and a point or object.

International Civil Aviation Organization Standard Atmosphere; The ICAO Standard Atmosphere is a reference model defining standard vertical profiles of temperature and pressure used for aviation and atmospheric calculations.
wind Shear; Wind shear is the change in wind speed and/or direction with height, diagnosed from model wind fields.
Isotherm 0 C; The 0 °C isotherm is the atmospheric level or line where the air temperature equals 0 degrees Celsius, indicating the freezing level.
0.33 To 1 Sigma Layer; The 0.33-1 sigma layer represents the lower atmospheric layer between 0.33 and 1.0 of normalized surface pressure, encompassing the boundary layer and lower troposphere.
0.995 Sigma Level; 0.995 sigma level is a terrain-following model level located at 99.5% of surface pressure, representing near-surface atmospheric conditions.
potential Temperature; The temperature a parcel of dry air would have if brought adiabatically (i.e., without transfer of heat or mass) to a standard pressure level of 1000 mbar.
Lifted Parcel Level; The height at which the relative humidity of an air parcel will reach 100% with respect to liquid water when it cooled by dry adiabatic lifting.
Illicit Drug; Illicit Drug Use includes the misuse of prescription psychotherapeutics or the use of marijuana, cocaine (including crack), heroin, hallucinogens, inhalants, or methamphetamine.
Hallucinogens; Includes LSD, PCP, Ecstasy and others.

Alcohol","Alcohol (Drug); Alcohol, sometimes referred to by the chemical name ethanol, is a psychoactive drug that is the active ingredient in drinks such as beer, wine, and distilled spirits (hard liquor).
Five Or More Drinks Once Or Twice AWeek

Binge Alcohol Use; Since 2015, binge alcohol use for females has been defined as drinking four or more drinks on the same occasion on at least 1 day in the past 30 days.
Heavy Alcohol Use; Heavy Alcohol Use is defined as binge drinking on the same occasion on each of 5 or more days in the past 30 days; all heavy alcohol users are also binge alcohol users.
