USDA_Large Metro; Large metropolitan statistical areas have a total population of 1 million or more.
USDA_Small Metro; Small metropolitan statistical areas have a total population of fewer than 1 million.

USDA_Nonmetro; Nonmetropolitan counties were classified according to the aggregate size of their urban population. Nonmetropolitan areas include counties in micropolitan statistical areas and counties outside of both metropolitan and micropolitan statistical areas and are classified as follows: \urbanized","\ \"less urbanized","\ and \"completely rural.\
USDA_Nonmetro Urbanized; Nonmetropolitan county with a population of 20,000 or more in urbanized areas.
USDA_Nonmetro Less Urbanized; Nonmetropolitan county with a population of at least 2,500 but fewer than 20,000 in urbanized areas.
USDA_Nonmetro Completely Rural; Nonmetropolitan county with a population of fewer than 2,500 in urbanized areas.

Cohort Set; Represents a set of entities that belong to the same cohort. A cohort may be cities that have stats in a particular import.

Provenance; A unique identifier for a data import, containing a set of structured records obtained from a data source. A Provenance may represent a subset of records contained in a (large) Dataset, or may represent a single Dataset. For example, 'dc/base/Census ACS5 Year Survey_Subject Tables_S1502' is one Provenance of the Dataset 'dc/d/United States Census Bureau_American Community Survey Acs' provided by the US Census Bureau, and represents one table of a database.
Curator; Identifies the person or organization that curated data.
ACLGroup; Represents a group of email IDs that have access to a resource.

Source; A unique identifier for the provider/owner of one or more imported datasets, normally an organization. For example, the Central Statistics Office of Ireland is represented by the Source 'dc/s/Central Statistics Office Cso Ireland'.

Dataset; A unique identifier for a collection of structured data records, obtained from a single Source. A Dataset may contain more than one Provenance, which is a subset of the records in the Dataset. For example, 'dc/base/Ireland_Census' is one Dataset from the Source 'dc/s/Central Statistics Office Cso Ireland'.

domain; The domain name of the Source that provided the data. For example, the domain of the Source 'dc/s/Central Statistics Office Cso Ireland' (representing the Central Statistics Office of Ireland) is 'cso.ie'. Compare with the URL for the Source, which is the full URL, e.g. 'https://www.cso.ie/en/index.html'.
import Time; The time at at which the Data Commons data processing pipeline was run.
automatic Refresh Schedule; The schedule for automatically updating a data import.
automatic Refresh Type; Represents how a data import is automatically updated in Data Commons.
pre Resolution Download Prefix Dir; Prefix of directory containing source files for auto-refresh.
pre Resolution Download Version File; Location of file containing the latest version directory name.
provenance Category; Category of data represented in a data import. Examples are observations for statistical variables and places/locales.
data Build Group; The data build that a given data import is part of.
earliest Data Import Date; Date of the initial import into Data Commons.
processing Method; Method by which the import artifacts were generated.
cached Source Data Url; Location of cached source data files.
entity Resolution Type; The type of entity resolution used for a data import.
last Data Refresh Date; The most recent date that the data was imported.
next Data Refresh Date; The date of the next scheduled data refresh.
resolved Text Mcf Url; Location of text MCF files for the provenance that have been aligned with the Knowledge Graph.
resolved Proto Mcf Url; Location of resolved proto MCF files for the provenance.

pre Resolution Table Url; Location of CSV files and corresponding Template-MCF file for the provenance that are not yet aligned with the Knowledge Graph. There should be one Template-MCF file with extension `.tmcf` and one or more CSV files or file patterns.
data Transformation Logic; Location of data transformation logic for importing the provenance.
next Source Release Date; The date of the next expected data release (publication) by the Source.
imported Source Release Date; Date that the data was released (published) by the Source. This is different from the last Data Refresh Date, which is the date the data is imported into Data Commons.
source Release Frequency; The data relese (publication) frequency by the Source, expressed in ISO 8601 duration format `P[0-9]*[Y|M|D|h|m|s]`.

source Data Url; The download URL of the imported source data. For example, the Provenance 'dc/base/Census ACS5 Year Survey_Subject Tables_S1502' is downloaded from 'https://data.census.gov/cedsci/table?q=S1502&tid=ACSST5 Y2023.S1502'. Compare with the Dataset URL, 'https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/acs', which is the web page hosting links to the downloads for the entire dataset.
earliest Observation Date; Date of the earliest observation in the current data import.
latest Observation Date; Date of the most recent observation in the current data import.

domain Includes; Specifies the entity type or types that can include this property in their schema definition. The value of domain Includes is a Class. For example, the property 'unit' has a domain Includes of 'Stat Var Observation'. This means that 'unit' can be used as a property in observations.

range Includes; Specifies the valid type or types for a property. The value of range Includes is a Class. It may be a primitive type such as 'Text' or 'Number' or 'Integer', or it may be a more complex type, such as a (previously defined) Class or Enum. For example, the property 'name' has a range Includes of 'Text'.

date Established; The date on which the Creative Work first became available for searching on an online database.
date Revised; The date on which the Creative Work was last edited or revised or the item was last revised on a Data Feed.
example; One of a number of things that belong together in a grouping.
minority Serving Institution Type; Category of a Minority Serving Institution, e.g. HBCU (Historically Black Colleges and Universities), TCU (Tribal Colleges and Universities).
college Or University Level; Level of a college or university, e.g. 2-year, 4-year.

central Index Key; A Central Index Key (CIK) is a unique 10-digit number assigned by the United States Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to individuals, companies, filing agents, or foreign governments.

employer Identification Number; The Employer Identification Number (EIN), also known as the Federal Employer Identification Number (FEIN) or the Federal Tax Identification Number, is a unique nine-digit number assigned by the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) to business entities operating in the United States for the purposes of identification. When the number is used for identification rather than employment tax reporting, it is usually referred to as a Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN).
incorporated In Place; Place where a company was incorporated

figi; Financial Instrument Global Identifier (FIGI) from Open FIGI, an open system for identifying instruments globally across all asset classes. A FIGI is a 12 character, alphanumeric, randomly generated ID that covers hundreds of millions of active and inactive instruments that never changes once assigned.

value Of Transactions; The total purchase price paid for the equity of the acquired entity plus debt assumed of the acquired entity as determined in good faith by the Board.
up Time; Uptime is a metric that represents the percentage of time that hardware, an IT system or device is successfully operational.
down Time; Down time is a metric that represents the percentage of time that hardware, an IT system or device is not successful.
recon Name; Name used to match entities in the Knowledge Graph.

Waste Water; Wastewater is used water. It includes substances such as human waste, food scraps, oils, soaps and chemicals
withdrawal Rate; The rate of withdrawing something from another thing.
usage Rate; The rate of using something.
water Type; The type of water, such as fresh water, saline water.
water Source; The source of water, such as ground water, surface water.
water Treatment Level; The treatment level/stage in the wastewater treatment plant
water Treatment Type; The method of the wastewater treatment
water Treatment Status; It says whether the wastewater is treated or non-treated
water Treatment System; The type of the system(independently in a small scale or privately/by government in a large scale)by which the waste water is treated
Fresh Water; Water that contains less than 1,000 milligrams per liter (mg/L) of dissolved solids.
Saline Water; Water that contains 1,000 mg/L or more of dissolved solids.
Ground Water; Water in the ground that fully saturates pores or cracks in soils and rocks.
Surface Water; Water flowing on the Earth's surface.

Tertiary Treatment; Tertiary water treatment is the final stage of the multi-stage wastewater cleaning process. This third stage of treatment removes inorganic compounds, bacteria, viruses, and parasites.
Waste Water Treatment Plant; Conventional wastewater treatment includes pretreatment, primary treatment, and secondary treatment in a treatment facility area.
Waste Water Treated; Treated wastewater can be reused as reclaimed water. The main purpose of wastewater treatment is for the treated wastewater to be able to be disposed or reused

Waste Water Treated; Untreated wastewater is also known as raw sewage. It's unsafe for disposal or reuse because it contains organic matter, chemicals, and pathogens. Disposing of it without treatment can seriously harm the environment and public health.

Waste Water Independently Treated; Home wastewater enters the septic tank, which separates solids from liquids. Solids are held in the septic tank and liquids are conveyed to the final soil treatment site. The septic tank is a “bioreactor” where microorganisms break down organic matter in the wastewater to liquids, gases and solids.
Waste Water Collecting System; Collection systems are the system of underground pipes and maintenance structures used to convey wastewater to a Water Resource Recovery Facility (WRRF)
Public Sewerage; Collection Systems are the system of underground pipes and maintenance structures used to convey wastewater to a Water Resource Recovery Facility (WRRF)
Secondary Treatment; Secondary treatment involves the removal of biodegradable organic matter (BOD) and suspended solids (TSS) through the processes of aeration and filtration.
Unspecified Treatment; The water treatments in the wastewater plant that are not specified
Public Sewerage Without Treatment; If sewage is discharged untreated, its nitrogen and phosphorus content can lead to pollution of lakes and reservoirs via a process called eutrophication
Primary Treatment; Primary treatment removes material that will either float or readily settle out by gravity
enrollment Program; A property that specifies a structured program in which a student is officially registered or participating.
Enrollment Program Enum; An enumeration of different types of structured programs or courses in which a student can be enrolled.
Gifted And Talented; Represents enrollment in programs designed for students identified as having outstanding abilities who need services not ordinarily provided by the regular school program.
Not Enrolled In Advanced Placement Exam; Indicates a student is not registered to take any Advanced Placement (AP) Exam.
US_Urban Disability Status Enum; An enumeration of disability statuses specific to the United States urban context, related to educational and service provision acts.

Disability Served Under Education Act; Indicates a student with a disability who is receiving special education and related services under a specific education act, such as the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act (IDEA).

Disability Served Under Section 50 4","Disability Served Under Section 504; Indicates a student with a disability who is receiving accommodations or services under Section 504 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973.
Enrolled In One Or More Advanced Placement Exam; Indicates a student is registered to take one or more, but not necessarily all, Advanced Placement (AP) Exams.
Enrolled In All Advanced Placement Exam; Indicates a student is registered to take every Advanced Placement (AP) Exam offered or relevant to their curriculum.
Advanced Placement Exam; Represents participation in the College Board's Advanced Placement (AP) program exams, which offer college-level curricula and examinations to high school students.
Failed In Advanced Placement Exam; Indicates a student received a non-passing score (e.g., typically a 1 or 2) on an Advanced Placement (AP) Exam.
Passed In One Or More Advanced Placement Exam; Indicates a student received a passing score (e.g., typically a 3, 4, or 5) on one or more, but not necessarily all, Advanced Placement (AP) Exams.
Passed In All Advanced Placement Exam; Indicates a student received a passing score (e.g., typically a 3, 4, or 5) on every Advanced Placement (AP) Exam they took.
Participated In Scholastic Assessment Test And American College Testing; Indicates a student took both the Scholastic Assessment Test (SAT) and the American College Testing (ACT) assessment exams.
student Type; A property that specifies the classification or category of the student.
Cohort Student; A student who is tracked as part of a specific group (cohort) for the purpose of longitudinal analysis, such as graduation rates.
minimum Rate; A property that represents the lowest rate or percentage observed or associated with a statistical variable or student metric.
maximum Rate; A property that represents the highest rate or percentage observed or associated with a statistical variable or student metric.
mean Rate; A property that represents the average rate or percentage observed or associated with a statistical variable or student metric.
Other Advanced Placement Course; A category representing any Advanced Placement (AP) course or subject not explicitly listed elsewhere.
International Baccalaureate Diploma; Represents participation in the International Baccalaureate (IB) Diploma Programme, a two-year educational program for students aged 16–19.
Computer Science; A school subject focused on the theoretical foundations of information and computation and their practical implementation and application.

Advanced Placement Language; A school subject related to the study of language through an Advanced Placement (AP) curriculum, such as AP English Language and Composition or a World Language AP course.
Algebra; An elementary school subject focusing on the branch of mathematics concerning the properties and relations of operations and the construction and analysis of sets and their elements.
Algebra 1; An introductory high-school level mathematics course focused on the foundational concepts of algebra, such as linear equations and inequalities.
educational Status; A property that specifies a student's current standing or condition within the educational system.
Retained; Indicates a student was held back and is repeating a grade level.
Educational Status Enum; An enumeration of different possible statuses or conditions a student can hold within the educational system.
Grades 9 To 10; A range representing students in the ninth and tenth grades of school (typically the first two years of high school).
Grades 11 To 12; A range representing students in the eleventh and twelfth grades of school (typically the last two years of high school).
Grades 7 To 8; A range representing students in the seventh and eighth grades of school (typically middle school or junior high).
United States_Proficient; Indicates a student has demonstrated competency in challenging subject matter knowledge and skills, typically as defined by a United States standardized assessment.
military Connected Status; A property that specifies the connection of a student to the military (e.g., student is a dependent of an active duty member).
Military Connected Status Enum; An enumeration of different statuses that define a student's connection to the military.
Military Connected; Indicates a student who is connected to the military, such as being the child or spouse of an active-duty service member.

Economic Metric Enum; Type of economic metric.

Real Value; Measurement is evaluated against the price of goods or services at a different time to adjust for inflation. This adjustment could be via a chained volume series (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chained_volume_series) or a fixed-weight method.
Nominal; Measurement is evaluated at current market prices, without adjusting for inflation.
Purchasing Power Parity; Represents prices of different areas using a common good or goods to contrast the real purchasing power between different currencies.
Emissions; The production and discharge of something.
Gas Type; Type of gas. For example, carbon dioxide.
consumed Thing; Type of consumption, such as energy.
produced Thing; Something that is produced, such as electricity.
consumer Age; Age of the person engaging in the usage or consumption of something.
Consumption; The usage or consumption of something.
Production; Production is a process of combining various material inputs and immaterial inputs (plans, know-how) in order to make something for consumption (output).
Electricity From Oil Gas Or Coal Sources; Electricity produced from oil, gas, or coal.
Electricity From Nuclear Sources; Electricity produced from nuclear sources.
Economic Activity; The activity of making, providing, purchasing, or selling goods or services.
Economic Activity Source Enum; The source of economic activity. For example, GDP & GNI.
Gross Domestic Production; A monetary measure of the market value of all the final goods and services produced in a specific time period.
Gross National Income; The total domestic and foreign output claimed by residents of a country.
Gross Value Added; The measure of the value of goods and services produced in an area, industry or sector of an economy.
Expenditure Activity; The outflow of money, or any form of fortune in general, to another person or group to pay for an item or service, or for a category of costs.
remunerator; The person or entity that pays or compensates.

Government; A government is the system or group of people governing an organized community, often a state, but also other entities, for example, a company. While all types of organizations have governance, the word government is often used more specifically to refer to the approximately 200 independent national governments on Earth, as well as their subsidiary organizations.
is Internet User; Represents whether a person is considered as an Internet user.

fertility Rate; The number of children that would be born to a woman if she were to live to the end of her childbearing years and bear children in accordance with age-specific fertility rates of the specified year.

gini Index; In economics, the Gini coefficient, sometimes called the Gini index or Gini ratio, is a measure of statistical dispersion intended to represent the income inequality or wealth inequality within a nation or any other group of people.
medical Condition; A medical condition is a broad term that includes all diseases and disorders.
Stock; Stock (also capital stock) of a corporation, is all of the shares into which ownership of the corporation is divided.

Remittance; A remittance is a transfer of money, often by a foreign worker to an individual in their home country. Money sent home by migrants competes with international aid as one of the largest financial inflows to developing countries.

Inward Remittance; Personal transfers and compensation of employees received by resident households. Compensation of employees refers to the income of border, seasonal, and other short-term workers who are employed in an economy where they are not resident and of residents employed by nonresident entities.

Outward Remittance; Personal transfers and compensation of employees made by resident households. Compensation of employees refers to the income of border, seasonal, and other short-term workers who are employed in an economy where they are not resident and of residents employed by nonresident entities.

Wasting; In medicine, wasting, also known as wasting syndrome, refers to the process by which a debilitating disease causes muscle and fat tissue to waste away. Wasting is sometimes referred to as "acute malnutrition" because it is believed that episodes of wasting have a short duration","in contrast to stunting","which is regarded as chronic malnutrition.

Severe Wasting; A severe condition of wasting, the process by which a debilitating disease causes muscle and fat tissue to waste away. Wasting is sometimes referred to as "acute malnutrition" because it is believed that episodes of wasting have a short duration","in contrast to stunting","which is regarded as chronic malnutrition.
Less Than5 Meters Above Sea Level; Describes a place that is less than 5 meters above sea level.

Live Birth; The complete expulsion or extraction from its mother of a product of conception, irrespective of the duration of the pregnancy, which, after such separation, breathes or shows any other evidence of life such as beating of the heart, pulsation of the umbilical cord, or definite movement of voluntary muscles, whether or not the umbilical cord has been cut or the placenta is attached.
Net Domestic Production; Net domestic product accounts for capital that has been consumed over the year in the form of housing, vehicle, or machinery deterioration.

Net Value Added; Net value added is obtained by deducting consumption of fixed capital (or depreciation charges) from gross value added. Net value added therefore equals gross wages, pre-tax profits net of depreciation, and indirect taxes less subsidies.
